summary: Finished matching the Target Test Ice Climbers ground/stage module by replacing the remaining asm object with the C object, deleting asm/melee/gr/grticeclimber.s, implementing grTIceClimber_80221208, and tightening related item/material prototypes and structs needed for the match. No PR body or review comments were present in the slice.

key_files.role: Deleted 478-line assembly fallback after the C translation matched.
Added includes and implemented grTIceClimber_80221208 with item target JObj access, effect/camera/ground/material calls, and PAD_STACK.
Updated grTIceClimber_80221208 prototype from void(void) to void(HSD_GObj*).
Replaced placeholder prototypes with typed HSD_GObj and HSD_GObjEvent signatures needed by the implementation.
Added itTargetVars with an HSD_JObj* field.
Added target variant to Item.xDD4_itemVar union.
Changed melee/gr/grticeclimber.c from NonMatching to Matching.
Swapped the object list from asm/melee/gr/grticeclimber.s.o to src/melee/gr/grticeclimber.c.o.

what_changed: Removed the assembly fallback file asm/melee/gr/grticeclimber.s, including text, data, and sdata2 definitions for the module.
Changed build metadata so grticeclimber.c is treated as Matching in configure.py and compiled from src in obj_files.mk.

grTIceClimber_80221208 now gets Item* via GET_ITEM(gobj), accesses it->xDD4_itemVar.target.jobj, sets JObj flags with HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll(..., 0x10), reads position with lb_8000B1CC, spawns effect 0x445, triggers Camera_80030E44(2, 0), calls Ground_801C53EC(310), then grMaterial_801C8CDC(gobj).
Added required includes in grticeclimber.c for camera, efsync, item inlines/types, lb_00B0, and baselib/jobj.
Updated grTIceClimber_80221208 declaration in grticeclimber.h to accept HSD_GObj*.
Typed grMaterial_801C8CDC as taking HSD_GObj* and grMaterial_801C8CFC as taking HSD_GObjEvent instead of placeholder/Event types.
Added itTargetVars to itCommonItems.h and added the target union member to Item.xDD4_itemVar.
smart_moves: The match was completed by fixing type information around the call chain rather than leaving placeholder prototypes: grMaterial_801C8CDC and grMaterial_801C8CFC were given concrete HSD types.
The implementation identified the gobj as an item and reused GET_ITEM plus a target-specific item variable union member, which avoids raw offset access to the JObj at xDD4.
The C implementation preserved the original stack-sensitive shape with PAD_STACK(16), a common matching tactic when local Vec3 layout and compiler stack allocation affect output.
The PR updated both configure.py and obj_files.mk in the same change, ensuring the source object actually replaces the deleted asm object in the build.
A small, narrowly scoped struct addition, itTargetVars, made the decompiled code readable while supplying the specific layout needed by the target item behavior.
decomp_lessons: When a stage/ground callback receives an HSD_GObj but manipulates target item data, model the item-specific xDD4_itemVar union member instead of forcing generic or placeholder access.
Finishing a match can require improving nearby headers: accurate function pointer/event types and HSD object types can be necessary for compiler-compatible call signatures.
For asm removal PRs, always update both the matching status in configure.py and the TEXT_O_FILES source-vs-asm path in obj_files.mk.
Stack padding may be necessary around local Vec3 temporaries when matching small callback functions that call into effects or position helpers.
Header prototypes should be corrected alongside source implementations; here the callback changed from void(void) to void(HSD_GObj*) to match its use as an HSD_GObjEvent-like callback.
naming_conventions: The module keeps doldecomp address-based stage names such as grTIceClimber_80221208 and grTIceClimber_80221288.
Static/data symbols retain the stage abbreviation prefix grTIc, such as grTIc_803E8A98 and grTIc_803E8B5C.
Item variant structs use lower camel/prefix style matching existing item headers, e.g. itTargetVars.
The Item union member is named target to match the added itTargetVars type.
Typed HSD callback signatures use HSD_GObjEvent rather than the less-specific Event placeholder.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted the complete asm/melee/gr/grticeclimber.s file after achieving a C match.
Changed obj_files.mk from building asm/melee/gr/grticeclimber.s.o to src/melee/gr/grticeclimber.c.o.
Used PAD_STACK(16) in grTIceClimber_80221208 to preserve matching stack layout.
Used a local Vec3 pos and passed its address to lb_8000B1CC and efSync_Spawn, matching the original callback's position-fetch/effect-spawn flow.
Used concrete constants visible in the asm-derived behavior: JObj flag 0x10, effect ID 0x445, camera arguments 2 and 0, and Ground_801C53EC argument 310.
follow_up_queries: Find other grt* Target Test stage modules still marked NonMatching in configure.py.
Search for other item target behaviors that should use itTargetVars or xDD4_itemVar.target.
Audit grMaterial prototypes for remaining placeholder or Event signatures.
Compare grTIceClimber_80221208 with similar target-hit callbacks in other Target Test modules.
Search for PAD_STACK use in small effect-spawn callbacks to reuse stack-layout tactics.
